I'm curious as to why do most hurricanes hit at night? Is it just me or is it a commonality?
I don't think it matters. There's been plenty of daytime hurricane landfalls. They don't just stop and wait for it to be nighttime to strike.
I'm wondering this as well
I always wonder that too! It is annoying I need to see what is happening. Piece of mind thing! Ugh

It's at night when the upper and middle part of the atmosphere cools (because the sun is not there to heat it up) and that releases energy in the storms, which turns into winds and moisture.
With the increased winds and moisture, storms become stronger, likely pushing them further along their paths toward land.
And there's no proven scientific correlation really and one of the worse Katrina blew in several years ago at 6:10 a.m.
